stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including 'transformers' and 'llama'.
The primary application pipeline associated with this model is 'text-generation'.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the core field of text generation using transformer-based models.
The model is associated with Meta's Llama family, indicating relevance in the large language model space.

stories.title: Would you let robots spend your money? Google is betting on it
stories.author: Mia Sato
bullet_points: Google is heavily investing in AI-driven shopping with a new 'Universal Cart' feature.
This Universal Cart aims to integrate shopping across various retailers and Google services (Gemini, YouTube, Gmail).
The move signals a major industry shift as Google pushes AI commerce while competitors reportedly hold back.

stories.title: Google Search is getting its biggest changes ever
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Google Search is undergoing a major AI evolution, highlighted at Google I/O 2026.
The new interface seamlessly integrates AI Overviews (summaries) and AI Mode (chatbot experience).
The updates are powered by the latest Gemini models, signaling a significant shift in search functionality.

stories.title: Gmail is going to start talking to you
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Google is introducing 'Gmail Live,' a new AI-powered voice mode for Gmail.
The feature leverages technology similar to Gemini Live, allowing users to interact with their inbox via voice.
Users can activate Gmail Live by tapping a new icon in the Gmail search bar.

stories.title: Google is launching its own version of OpenClaw
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Google is launching 'Gemini Spark,' its proprietary AI agent platform, as a direct competitor to OpenClaw.
Gemini Spark is designed as an 'always-on' AI agent capable of performing complex, proactive tasks.
Key functionalities include drafting emails, generating dynamic study guides, and monitoring financial statements for hidden fees.

stories.title: Google announces agent-optimized Gemini 3.5.Flash and a do-anything model called Omni
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google introduced Gemini 3.5 Flash, emphasizing its efficiency for agent-optimized AI applications.
The announcement includes 'Omni,' described as a versatile, 'do-anything' model from Google.
The focus on 'agentic AI' signals a major industry shift toward autonomous AI systems.

stories.title: Gemini 3.5 Flash
stories.author: spectraldrift
bullet_points: Google has released Gemini 3.5 Flash, indicating a new iteration in their large language model family.
The release suggests a focus on optimizing for speed and efficiency ('Flash') within Google's AI offerings.
The announcement points to continued, rapid development and iteration within the competitive AI landscape.

stories.title: I’ve joined Anthropic
stories.author: dmarcos
bullet_points: Key AI researcher Andrej Karpathy has joined Anthropic.
This move is significant as it involves a prominent figure in the AI/ML community joining a major AI lab.
The news suggests potential shifts or increased focus within the competitive landscape of large language model development (Anthropic vs. OpenAI).

stories.title: Gemini 3.5 Flash Developer Guide
stories.author: pat_loeber
bullet_points: Gemini 3.5 Flash is now generally available (GA).
The model is reported to be stable and production-ready.
The release targets scaled production use cases for developers.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the DeepSeek-R1 model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a large language model.
The associated tags suggest relevance to transformer architectures and the DeepSeek family of models.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance gains.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads for Apple hardware.
This is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy and speed.
The core value proposition is connecting messaging workflows with local AI capabilities.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various coding models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is collaborating with OpenAI and ROOST to integrate gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models.
These models are designed for safety classification tasks and are available in 20B and 120B parameter sizes.
The models are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting open-source safety tooling.

stories.title: Gemini will use Volvo’s external cameras to interpret parking signs
stories.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
bullet_points: Google's Gemini AI is integrating with Volvo vehicles to enhance situational awareness.
The AI will utilize external vehicle cameras to interpret and explain surroundings, such as parking signs.
This partnership showcases the growing convergence of advanced AI with automotive technology.

stories.title: The 13 biggest announcements at Google I/O 2026
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Google I/O 2026 featured major AI announcements, including the launch of Gemini 3.5.
New features were rolled out for core Google services like Search and Gmail.
Updates were provided regarding Google's Project Aura smart glasses.

stories.title: Google wants to compete with Anthropic’s Mythos
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: Google is significantly increasing its focus and external availability of its cybersecurity offerings.
The company is pushing its 'CodeMender,' an AI agent designed specifically for code security.
This move signals Google's intent to compete more directly in the specialized AI security space, potentially against rivals like Anthropic.

stories.title: Google can now vibe-code you an Android app
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Google has enhanced its AI Studio platform to enable native Android app development via prompting.
Users can now input an app idea and preview the resulting application using an embedded Android emulator within AI Studio.
This represents a significant step in democratizing app creation through generative AI.

stories.title: Code as Agent Harness
stories.author: Jingrui He
bullet_points: Code is evolving from just an output of LLMs to a core operational substrate in agentic systems.
The concept of 'code as agent harness' unifies how code is used for agent reasoning, action, and environment modeling.
This research addresses the growing role of code in enabling advanced, execution-based verification within AI agents.

stories.title: ESI-Bench: Towards Embodied Spatial Intelligence that Closes the Perception-Action Loop
stories.author: Yejin Choi
bullet_points: Introduces ESI-Bench, a new benchmark focusing on Embodied Spatial Intelligence.
Shifts the paradigm from passive observation to active perception-action loops, where agents explore to uncover hidden information.
Addresses limitations of prior models by making the observer an active actor rather than relying on oracle observations.

stories.title: Predictable Confabulations: Factual Recall by LLMs Scales with Model Size and Topic Frequency
stories.author: Tegawendé F. Bissyandé
bullet_points: The research introduces a new scaling law linking factual recall in LLMs to both model size and topic frequency in training data.
Factual recall quality was found to follow a sigmoid relationship based on the log-linear combination of model parameters and topic representation.
The study analyzed 38 models across 8,900 scholarly references, demonstrating that model size and data composition explain 60% of the variance in recall.

stories.title: ESI-Bench: Towards Embodied Spatial Intelligence that Closes the Perception-Action Loop
stories.author: Yejin Choi
bullet_points: Introduces ESI-Bench, a new benchmark focusing on embodied spatial intelligence.
Proposes moving beyond passive sensing by recasting the observer as an active actor in a perception-action loop.
Aims to enable agents to actively uncover unseen information like occluded structure and dynamics.

stories.title: Predictable Confabulations: Factual Recall by LLMs Scales with Model Size and Topic Frequency
stories.author: Tegawendé F. Bissyandé
bullet_points: The research introduces a new scaling law linking factual recall in LLMs to both model size and the frequency of topics in the training data.
Factual recall quality was found to follow a sigmoid curve based on the log-linear combination of model parameters and topic representation.
The study analyzed 38 models across 8,900 scholarly references, demonstrating that model size and data composition explain 60% of the variance in recall.

stories.title: EnvFactory: Scaling Tool-Use Agents via Executable Environments Synthesis and Robust RL
stories.author: Zhijiang Guo
bullet_points: Introduces 'EnvFactory' to address scalability and robustness issues in training LLM tool-use agents.
Overcomes limitations of current methods, such as reliance on costly real-world APIs or hallucination-prone simulators.
Focuses on synthesizing executable environments to generate realistic, complex training data for Agentic RL.

stories.title: Code as Agent Harness
stories.author: Jingrui He
bullet_points: LLMs are evolving beyond just code generation to becoming integral parts of agentic systems.
Code is now viewed as an 'operational substrate' for agent reasoning, action, and environment modeling.
The paper introduces the concept of 'code as agent harness' to unify this functional shift in AI agents.

stories.title: Generative AI Advertising as a Problem of Trustworthy Commercial Intervention
stories.author: Qiaozhu Mei
bullet_points: Generative AI is fundamentally changing advertising by enabling interventions directly within the generative process of LLMs.
Current deployed systems maintain a visible boundary between commercial content and AI output, but this is often bypassed in practice.
The core problem identified is that ads woven into LLM outputs operate through less observable channels, posing a trust and transparency issue.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under core ML/NLP tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline demonstrated is 'text-generation', indicating a focus on generative AI capabilities.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Google Pics is a new app that tries to fix AI image editing
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Google is introducing 'Pics,' a new AI image generation app integrated into Workspace.
The app aims to improve the AI image editing workflow by reducing iteration hassle.
A key feature allows users to modify small aspects of an image via clicking, rather than rewriting the entire prompt.

stories.title: Electrical utility megamerger is all about the data centers
stories.author: Dan Gearino, Amy Green, and Charles Paullin, Inside Climate News
bullet_points: The news centers on a major merger involving an electrical utility (NextEra and Dominion).
The core driver of this megamerger is the increasing demand and infrastructure needs of data centers.
The article suggests the merger could lead to increased electricity costs for consumers.

stories.title: In addition to space stations, Vast says it will now build high-power satellites
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: Vast is expanding its business scope beyond space stations to include high-power satellite construction.
The company emphasizes the industry trend of diversification among successful space ventures.
This signals a potential shift in the commercial space sector towards multi-faceted service providers.

stories.title: Iran demands Big Tech pay fees for undersea Internet cables in Strait of Hormuz
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Iran is demanding fees from major technology companies for using undersea internet cables in the Strait of Hormuz.
This geopolitical pressure is forcing US tech companies to explore alternative, overland fiber routes.
The situation highlights the vulnerability of global internet infrastructure to regional political disputes.

stories.title: DashAttention: Differentiable and Adaptive Sparse Hierarchical Attention
stories.author: Marcos V. Treviso
bullet_points: Introduces DashAttention, a new model for hierarchical attention designed to overcome limitations in existing methods like NSA and InfLLMv2.
Addresses the critical flaw in current sparse attention methods where the top-k selection assumes a fixed number of relevant tokens.
Key innovation is making the sparse-to-dense transition differentiable, allowing for proper gradient flow between attention stages.

stories.title: Vision-OPD: Learning to See Fine Details for Multimodal LLMs via On-Policy Self-Distillation
stories.author: Yaojie Lu
bullet_points: Vision-OPD addresses the fine-grained visual understanding weakness in Multimodal LLMs (MLLMs).
The research identifies a 'regional-to-global perception gap,' where MLLMs perform better with cropped evidence than full images.
The core problem is suggested to be difficulty in focusing on relevant evidence, rather than just poor local recognition.

stories.title: What Does the AI Doctor Value? Auditing Pluralism in the Clinical Ethics of Language Models
stories.author: Gabriel A. Brat
bullet_points: The paper addresses the inherent pluralism and ethical conflicts within medical practice.
It examines the lack of systematic examination regarding the ethical values LLMs bring to medical advice.
The authors propose a framework specifically designed for auditing value pluralism in clinical ethics.

stories.title: DexHoldem: Playing Texas Hold'em with Dexterous Embodied System
stories.author: Yi Ma
bullet_points: Introduces DexHoldem, a new real-world benchmark for evaluating embodied AI systems.
The benchmark uses Texas Hold'em manipulation with a ShadowHand to test complex, multi-step agent capabilities.
It moves beyond isolated skills, requiring agents to perceive, act contextually, and maintain scene usability.

stories.title: Semantic Generative Tuning for Unified Multimodal Models
stories.author: Yanwei Li
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge in Unified Multimodal Models (UMMs) where understanding and generation are trained independently.
Identifies that decoupled training leads to misaligned representation spaces, hindering mutual reinforcement.
Introduces a new systematic investigation into 'Semantic Generative Tuning' to unify these processes.

stories.title: Distilling Tabular Foundation Models for Structured Health Data
stories.author: Pratinav Seth
bullet_points: The research addresses the high inference cost of Tabular Foundation Models (TFMs) used in health data.
It proposes using knowledge distillation to transfer the predictive power of large TFMs to smaller, lightweight models.
A novel technique, stratified out-of-fold teacher labeling, is introduced to prevent context leakage during distillation.

stories.title: DashAttention: Differentiable and Adaptive Sparse Hierarchical Attention
stories.author: Marcos V. Treviso
bullet_points: Introduces DashAttention, a new model for hierarchical attention designed to overcome limitations in existing methods like NSA and InfLLMv2.
Addresses the key flaw in current sparse attention methods: the fixed top-k selection prevents proper gradient flow between sparse and dense attention stages.
Proposes a differentiable and adaptive approach to hierarchical attention, allowing for more flexible and trainable token selection.

stories.title: Vision-OPD: Learning to See Fine Details for Multimodal LLMs via On-Policy Self-Distillation
stories.author: Yaojie Lu
bullet_points: Multimodal LLMs (MLLMs) exhibit a 'regional-to-global perception gap,' struggling with fine-grained visual understanding.
The model performs better on evidence-centered image crops than on the full image, indicating a focus/attention issue rather than pure recognition failure.
The paper proposes 'Vision-OPD' to address this gap using on-policy self-distillation to improve fine-detail perception in MLLMs.

stories.title: PIXLRelight: Controllable Relighting via Intrinsic Conditioning
stories.author: Ronald Clark
bullet_points: PIXLRelight introduces a new feed-forward approach for physically controllable single-image relighting.
It addresses limitations in existing methods, such as poor lighting control or error accumulation in rendering chains.
The core innovation is using a shared intrinsic conditioning to bridge Physically Based Rendering (PBR) and learned image synthesis.

stories.title: General Preference Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: John M. Cioffi
bullet_points: Current LLM alignment is split between online RL (good for verifiable tasks like math/code) and preference optimization (good for open-ended generation).
Online RL is limited by the need for a programmatic verifier, which fails on open-ended tasks.
The paper suggests the key challenge is developing a verifier for open-ended quality, noting that scalar reward models are insufficient.

stories.title: Learned Memory Attenuation in Sage-Husa Kalman Filters for Robust UAV State Estimation
stories.author: Marcin Żugaj
bullet_points: Addresses limitations of classical Kalman filters in UAV state estimation due to dynamic environmental noise and telemetry outages.
Introduces the N-Deep Recurrent Sage-Husa Filter (NDR-SHKF) to overcome the static forgetting factor constraint of the SHKF.
Focuses on improving robustness and balancing stability versus transient responsiveness in complex aerial robotics applications.

stories.title: DashAttention: Differentiable and Adaptive Sparse Hierarchical Attention
stories.author: Marcos V. Treviso
bullet_points: Introduces DashAttention, a new method for hierarchical attention in NLP models.
Addresses a limitation in existing methods (like NSA and InfLLMv2) where the top-k selection prevents gradient flow.
Proposes a differentiable and adaptive approach to sparse hierarchical attention, improving model training.

stories.title: ESI-Bench: Towards Embodied Spatial Intelligence that Closes the Perception-Action Loop
stories.author: Yejin Choi
bullet_points: Introduces ESI-Bench, a new benchmark focusing on Embodied Spatial Intelligence.
Proposes moving beyond passive sensing by framing the observer as an active actor in a perception-action loop.
Aims to allow agents to uncover unseen information, such as occluded structures and dynamics, which passive sensing misses.

stories.title: Vision-OPD: Learning to See Fine Details for Multimodal LLMs via On-Policy Self-Distillation
stories.author: Yaojie Lu
bullet_points: Multimodal LLMs (MLLMs) exhibit a 'regional-to-global perception gap' when handling fine-grained visual details.
The model performs better on evidence-centered image crops than on the full image, suggesting a focus/attention issue rather than pure recognition failure.
The proposed method, Vision-OPD, aims to improve MLLM performance by learning to focus on decisive, fine-grained visual evidence via self-distillation.

stories.title: Predictable Confabulations: Factual Recall by LLMs Scales with Model Size and Topic Frequency
stories.author: Tegawendé F. Bissyandé
bullet_points: The research introduces a new scaling law linking factual recall in LLMs to both model size and the frequency of topics in the training data.
Factual recall quality was found to follow a sigmoid curve based on the log-linear combination of model parameters and topic representation.
The study analyzed 38 models against over 8,900 scholarly references, showing that model size and topic composition explain 60% of the variance.

stories.title: General Preference Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: John M. Cioffi
bullet_points: LLM alignment is currently split between online RL (good for verifiable tasks like math/code) and preference optimization (good for open-ended generation).
Online RL is limited by the need for a programmatic verifier that struggles with open-ended tasks.
The research identifies the need for a verifier capable of assessing open-ended quality to bridge the gap between the two alignment methods.

stories.title: EnvFactory: Scaling Tool-Use Agents via Executable Environments Synthesis and Robust RL
stories.author: Zhijiang Guo
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations in scaling LLM tool-use agents, specifically the lack of robust and scalable execution environments.
It tackles the scarcity of realistic training data, noting that current methods rely on expensive real-world APIs or flawed simulators.
The proposed 'EnvFactory' aims to overcome these issues by synthesizing executable environments for Agentic Reinforcement Learning (Agentic RL).

stories.title: GIM: Evaluating models via tasks that integrate multiple cognitive domains
stories.author: Steven McClain
bullet_points: Introduces GIM (Grounded Integration Measure), a new benchmark for evaluating Large Language Models (LLMs).
GIM addresses limitations in current benchmarks (like GPQA/HLE and ARC-AGI) by moving beyond simple knowledge recall or abstract reasoning.
The benchmark focuses on tasks that integrate multiple cognitive domains, aiming for a more holistic assessment of model capability.

stories.title: Google changes its search box
stories.author: berkeleyjunk
bullet_points: Google is reportedly integrating AI, specifically Gemini, into its search bar.
The change represents a significant evolution in how Google processes and presents search results.
The news is being discussed across major tech and business publications.

stories.title: Show HN: Superlog (YC P26) – Observability that installs itself and fixes bugs
stories.author: Magnanten
bullet_points: Superlog is a new observability tool designed to be self-installing and self-healing.
Its core feature is an agent that automatically investigates errors and opens pull requests for fixes.
The tool aims to simplify observability setup through a daily wizard and automated maintenance.

stories.title: AI agents are only as useful as the tools they can safely touch
stories.author: jenueldev
bullet_points: The future of AI coding emphasizes safe and controlled tool access for AI agents.
Anthropic's Stainless acquisition and Claude Code updates highlight this trend.
Key developments include scoped credentials, sandboxes, and improved developer workflows.
